title: PromotionGate integration: operator review required before any promotion
goal: Wire the self-improvement loop's AcceptRejectDecision stage through the KERNEL-001 PromotionGate so any Accept (a) requires operator review evidence before the editable surface mutation is applied to authority and (b) emits a typed promotion receipt that EventLedger replays can reconstruct. No promotion proceeds without operator signoff per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS ('PromotionGate operator review per promotion').
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/promotion_gate_adapter.rs (LoopPromotionGate + PromotionRequest + PromotionApproval + PromotionRejection types). Wiring uses existing KERNEL-001 PromotionGate trait surface; no new gate. Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/loop_promotion_gate_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-148 (loop core), MT-149 (editable surface), MT-152 (promotion floor). KERNEL-001 PromotionGate exists in main.
implementation_notes: Per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS: 'PromotionGate operator review per promotion'. Per refinement D.2: 'promotion-gated; operator review required before promotion'. Surface: `pub struct LoopPromotionGate<'a> { gate: &'a dyn PromotionGateSubmitter }` with `pub fn submit(request: PromotionRequest) -> Result<PromotionTicket, GateError>` and `pub fn poll(ticket: &PromotionTicket) -> Result<PromotionStatus, GateError>`. PromotionRequest: `{ iteration_id, target: LoopTarget, baseline_snapshot, proposed_snapshot, eval_result: EvalResult, floor_decision: PromotionDecision, sentinel_decision: SentinelDecision, justification_text: String }`. PromotionStatus: `{ Pending { submitted_at_utc }, Approved { approval: PromotionApproval }, Rejected { rejection: PromotionRejection } }`. PromotionApproval: `{ approved_by: OperatorId, approved_at_utc, signoff_evidence_id: Uuid v7 }`. PromotionRejection: `{ rejected_by: OperatorId, rejected_at_utc, rejection_reason: String }`. The gate is asynchronous (submit then poll) because operator review may take hours/days; the loop scheduler (MT-156) tracks pending tickets and resumes the iteration ONLY after Approved. On Rejected, the loop records the rejection as a memory entry (so future iterations learn) and advances to the next target. Tests: submit creates a typed request via the gate; poll returns Pending immediately after submit; gate rejection produces typed PromotionRejection; gate approval populates signoff_evidence_id; loop cannot apply the surface mutation without an Approved status (assert by attempting apply with Pending status returns typed error).
risk_if_missed: Without PromotionGate integration the loop can apply Accept decisions directly to authority, bypassing operator review and creating a shadow self-modification path.
validator_focus: PromotionRequest captures the full evidence bundle (baseline + proposal + eval + floor + sentinel). Apply path is gated by PromotionStatus::Approved.

lifecycle.claim_note: MT-154 PromotionGate adapter integration; promotion_gate_adapter.rs already on disk from prior session with 4 inline tests; this claim adds the contract-named integration test file tests/loop_promotion_gate_tests.rs with adversarial coverage beyond minimum_controls. Deps MT-148 + MT-149 + MT-152 all RFV in parallel session.

implementation_record.summary: MT-154 PromotionGate adapter is on disk (src/self_improve/promotion_gate_adapter.rs, ~10K, authored prior session) with 4 inline #[cfg(test)] tests already covering submit -> Pending, require_approved Pending -> Approved transition with UUIDv7 signoff_evidence_id, require_approved Rejected typed error with rationale, and UnknownTicket typed error for forged ticket_id. This session added the contract-named integration test file tests/loop_promotion_gate_tests.rs (29 tests) covering: apply-gate enforcement (Pending / Rejected / Approved typed transitions, status-poll stability across repeated polls), forged-ticket defense (UnknownTicket on synthesized ticket_id direct + via require_approved), MT-149 ForbiddenSurfaceGuard short-circuits before submit (spec / role / lora variants assert gate.submit_count() stays at 0 when guard rejects), MT-152 PromotionFloor Rejected still submittable for audit transparency, MT-153 SentinelDecision::Pause carried in request bundle, request bundle completeness (all 5 evidence pieces + iteration_id + target + justification present in serialized JSON), request byte-for-byte serde round-trip preserving every field, audit metadata serde round-trip (PromotionApproval who/when/signoff_evidence_id; PromotionRejection who/when/reason; PromotionStatus all three variants; PromotionTicket; GateError all four variants), idempotency / dedup (3 identical submits yield 3 distinct ticket_ids while preserving the shared iteration_id linkage), cross-ticket-replay defense (approving ticket A leaves sibling ticket B Pending), concurrency (8 concurrent submits + 16 concurrent polls produce consistent typed observations without panic / race), I/O error propagation (typed GateError::Io for submit + poll + require_approved), helper-method semantics (is_approved / is_pending discriminant accuracy), and worst-case audit bundle (floor Rejected + sentinel Pause carried together still submittable + faithfully round-trips). 29/29 integration pass + 4/4 inline pass.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS - promotion_gate_adapter.rs is a complete impl with no '*Unavailable' / 'pending MT-NNN' / 'deferred to follow-on' patterns; trait + concrete adapter + typed errors + helper methods all shipped.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS - integration tests exercise the public LoopPromotionGate API via a MockGate implementing PromotionGateSubmitter; the public trait surface is the same one production wires to KERNEL-001 PromotionGate.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS - promotion_gate_adapter.rs authored in prior session (~10K); integration tests authored in this session; both differ from any validator role.

red_team_coverage.apply_path_gated_by_promotion_status_app: covered - mt154_require_approved_returns_pending_typed_error_immediately_after_submit + mt154_require_approved_returns_rejected_typed_error_with_rationale + mt154_require_approved_returns_typed_approval_with_signoff_evidence_uuidv7 prove all three status -> require_approved outcomes are typed; mt154_full_lifecycle_pending_then_approved_then_remains_approved proves the Approved status is sticky across repeated polls (no retroactive demotion).
red_team_coverage.promotion_request_bundles_all_evidence_b: covered - mt154_request_bundles_all_five_evidence_pieces_in_serialized_form asserts the serialized JSON contains baseline_snapshot / proposed_snapshot / eval_result / floor_decision / sentinel_decision / justification_text / target / iteration_id; mt154_request_round_trips_byte_for_byte_via_serde proves byte-equal round-trip so EventLedger replay can reconstruct what the reviewer saw; mt154_request_can_carry_floor_rejected_and_sentinel_pause_simultaneously proves the bundle preserves negative-signal evidence pieces.
red_team_coverage.rejection_recorded_as_memory_so_future_i: PARTIAL - PromotionRejection carries rejected_by / rejected_at_utc / rejection_reason and round-trips via serde so the loop can persist it as memory; the actual memory-write pathway lives in MT-156 scheduler. This MT proves the typed rejection shape; the downstream consumer integration is MT-156 scope.

residual_risks: MockGate is in-process; production wiring to KERNEL-001 PromotionGate transport (durable promotion-receipt store) is not exercised here. The LoopPromotionGate trait surface is the same one production will wire, but the durability + transport layer is downstream MT scope (likely shared with MT-156 scheduler).

Memory-persistence of rejections so future iterations don't re-propose the same change is a downstream MT-156 scheduler responsibility; this MT proves the typed PromotionRejection shape and serde round-trip but not the actual memory-write pathway.

Idempotency / dedup is intentionally left to the caller (loop scheduler) rather than enforced by the gate, so multiple submits on the same iteration_id yield distinct ticket_ids. A poorly written scheduler could spam the gate; this is acknowledged but not enforced at the adapter layer (audit history is the priority).

Concurrency tests run 8 + 16 workers in std::thread; production async runtime (tauri) was not exercised here, but the adapter is sync-trait and safe to call from any thread. If the production gate transport requires async, that wrapping is downstream.

FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION emission is not asserted here (no FEMS subscriber); the kind constant is referenced in MT-155 ipc.rs. Validator should verify the production loop emits this event when require_approved returns Approved or Rejected.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-154.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Apply path is gated by PromotionStatus::Approved; Pending or Rejected returns typed error.
PromotionRequest bundles all evidence (baseline + proposal + eval + floor + sentinel) so operator review is informed.
Rejection is recorded as memory so future iterations do not re-propose the same change.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: LoopPromotionGate adapter ships the contracted PromotionRequest/PromotionTicket/PromotionStatus/PromotionApproval/PromotionRejection/GateError types. Evidence bundle (baseline + proposal + eval + floor + sentinel + justification) is preserved and serde round-trips byte-for-byte per builder evidence. require_approved short-circuits Pending and Rejected with typed errors. 29 integration + 4 inline tests reported. HIGH-severity adversarial gap: the trait surface and the MockGate test fixture do NOT bind PromotionTicket.iteration_id to PromotionRequest.iteration_id — the gate is free to return a ticket bearing any iteration_id, breaking the audit linkage. Production code must validate this binding before trusting downstream loop wiring.
